text.title: Pair Programming Roles and Modes
text.summary: Driver and Navigator responsibilities, four pairing modes, and AI-navigator adaptation of Strong-Style.

list.item: Writes the code — focuses on tactical, line-by-line implementation
Verbalizes thought process ("I'm creating a validator class here because...")
Asks navigator for input when stuck
Switches roles when tired or blocked
Reviews code in real-time — spots bugs, typos, edge cases
Thinks strategically about architecture and design
Keeps track of next steps and open questions
Guides, does not dictate — suggests, does not demand

section.title: Classic Driver-Navigator
section.p: Driver codes, navigator reviews. Switch every 15-30 minutes. Best for most situations and complex features.

section.title: Ping-Pong (TDD)
section.p: Person A writes a failing test. Person B makes it pass. Person B writes the next failing test. Person A makes it pass. Best for TDD practice and keeping both engaged.

rule.statement: In Strong-Style, an idea must go through the other person's hands to reach the computer. The navigator dictates; the driver types. The driver must understand before typing.
rule.rationale: Forces the navigator to articulate clearly; driver learns by doing. Best for mentoring and knowledge transfer.

section.title: Tour Guide
section.p: Expert guides newcomer through the codebase; newcomer drives. Expert explains architecture, history, and gotchas. Best for onboarding new team members.

section.title: AI-Navigator Adaptation

rule.statement: When pairing with an AI agent as navigator, the human is always physically the driver. The agent must emit ONE bounded instruction per turn, explain why, and wait for the human result before the next instruction.
rule.rationale: Multi-step agent instructions degrade into agent-driven solo coding, removing the human from the loop. One instruction at a time maintains the pairing dynamic.

rule.statement: Restate Strong-Style rules every ~10 turns when using an AI navigator. Under length pressure, agents revert to emitting full files.
rule.rationale: Agent context degrades over long sessions; explicit re-statement of the mode contract prevents role drift.

section.reference: System prompt for Strong-Style AI navigator mode.
